name: Tower Bridge
summary: Tower Bridge is a combined bascule and suspension bridge crossing the River Thames in London, built between 1886 and 1894. Designed by Sir Horace Jones and engineered by Sir John Wolfe Barry, its distinctive twin Gothic towers and opening roadway make it one of London's most recognizable landmarks. The bridge was constructed to ease road traffic while maintaining river access to the busy Pool of London docks.

timeline.title: Construction Begins
timeline.description: Construction of Tower Bridge begins on April 21, 1886, following a design competition won by Sir Horace Jones. Over 11,000 tons of steel are used to create the framework, which is then clad in Cornish granite and Portland stone to complement the nearby Tower of London. The project employs 432 workers daily.

timeline.title: Official Opening
timeline.description: Tower Bridge is officially opened on June 30, 1894 by the Prince and Princess of Wales. The bridge features two massive bascules that can be raised to allow tall ships to pass through, powered by steam-driven hydraulic engines. In its first year, the bascules are raised approximately 6,160 times.

timeline.title: Walkways Controversy
timeline.description: The high-level walkways connecting the two towers, originally designed for pedestrians to cross when the bascules were raised, are closed due to their poor reputation. They become notorious as a haunt for pickpockets and prostitutes, as few pedestrians bother to climb the 200 steps when the bridge is rarely fully open.

timeline.title: Blitz Damage
timeline.description: During the London Blitz of World War II, German bombs damage the bridge's north tower and destroy the original steam-powered hydraulic machinery. Despite the damage, the bridge remains operational, a crucial lifeline for war-torn London. Emergency repairs keep the bascules functioning throughout the war.

timeline.title: Bus Jumping Incident
timeline.description: On December 30, 1952, as the bridge's bascules begin to open, a double-decker bus driven by Albert Gunter is forced to accelerate and jump the gap as the road rises ahead of it. The bus lands safely on the other side, and Gunter later receives a reward of 10 pounds for his quick thinking.

timeline.title: Conversion to Electric Hydraulics
timeline.description: The bridge's original steam-powered hydraulic system, after decades of service, is replaced with a modern electro-hydraulic drive system. The new system uses oil and electricity rather than steam, dramatically improving reliability and efficiency while preserving the bridge's iconic opening mechanism.

timeline.title: Olympic Rings Display
timeline.description: During the London 2012 Olympic Games, massive Olympic rings are hung from Tower Bridge as part of the city's celebrations. The bridge undergoes a major renovation including repainting in red, white, and blue, and the high-level glass-floor walkways are reopened to the public as a major tourist attraction.
